item.title: Air Canada Crew Member Survives Ejection in Crash ‘Miracle’
item.description: A flight attendant on the Air Canada Express plane that crashed into a fire truck at New York’s LaGuardia Airport Sunday was found alive on the tarmac after being ejected meters away — and still attached to her seat.

item.title: Cliffwater’s Father-Son Duo Is Lashed by Private Credit’s Brewing Storm
item.description: Stephen and Blake Nesbitt built their modest consulting business into a private credit giant. Now their industry connections are being scrutinized as investors rush for the exits.

item.title: Oil Market Is ‘Singularly Focused’ on Hormuz, Vitol Says
item.description: Oil markets are currently pricing in the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz sooner rather than later, according to Ben Marshall, CEO of Vitol Group in the Americas.

item.title: Anti-Migrant Push Is Spurring Chile’s Employers to Vet Payrolls
item.description: Fear is coursing through the ranks of Chile’s undocumented immigrants as the new government signals a clampdown on employers that hire foreigners without full paperwork.

item.title: Citadel Securities Sees Markets Shifting to ‘Demand Destruction’
item.description: The market fallout from the Middle East conflict is entering a new phase, with investors starting to shift their focus from the initial inflation shock to the hit on global economic growth, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: GPA Creditors Tap Moelis as Adviser Amid Debt Restructuring
item.description: Local bondholders of GPA hired Moelis & Co as a financial adviser following the Brazilian supermarket chain’s decision to restructure its debt in an out-of-court proceeding, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Trump’s Wild, 5-Minute Rally Sends Clear Message to Wall Street
item.description: Just moments after Donald Trump backed down from his threat to bomb Iran’s energy infrastructure in a Truth Social post at 7:05 a.m., oil prices plunged over 13%, Treasury yields tumbled and traders signaled that US stocks would surge at the opening bell.

item.title: Spotify Lays Off 15 People, About 3% of Podcasting Staff
item.description: Spotify Technology SA laid off 15 people, approximately 3% of its podcasting team, as the company reorganizes to create fewer layers of management and faster decision-making.

item.title: Trump Wants Powell Out, But What Happens If Warsh Isn't Confirmed in Time?
item.description: The ongoing legal standoff between the Trump administration and Federal Reserve is increasing the likelihood that the next Fed chief won’t be confirmed by the Senate before Chair Jerome Powell’s term ends in May.

item.title: Renewable Energy Firm Boralex Explores Going Private
item.description: Canadian renewable energy firm Boralex Inc. is weighing its options including a potential deal to go private, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Hedge Fund Founder Weiss Loses Defamation Suit Against Jefferies
item.description: Hedge fund founder George Weiss has lost his defamation suit accusing Jefferies Financial Group of running a “smear campaign” to pressure him to pay a $100 million debt owed by his firm, Weiss Multi-Strategy Advisers.

item.title: Dutch Finance Ministry Blocks Computer Systems After Hack
item.description: The Dutch Finance Ministry said it had blocked access to some of its computer systems after a hack was detected on March 19.

item.title: Silicon Valley’s DC Summit Shadowed by AI Concerns, War in Iran
item.description: Silicon Valley’s alliance with the Trump administration will be on display Tuesday at a summit in Washington where business leaders’ calls to keep the US lead in artificial intelligence will be tested by growing public concern over AI’s economic impact and its use in the Iran war.

item.title: NYC Subway Project’s $1 Billion Contract Hinges on Frozen Funds
item.description: New York City’s transit agency is set to approve a $1 billion excavation contract as part of its Second Avenue subway expansion — but the deal might be stalled if federal funds for the project are not released.

item.title: Activist Ubben’s Inclusive Capital Looks to Sell Stake in Bayer
item.description: Inclusive Capital Partners is looking to sell a stake in Bayer AG, three years after the investment firm led by activist Jeff Ubben first disclosed a holding.

item.title: Trump’s Energy Boss Plays Down Price Shock From Iran Conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s energy chief downplayed the impact the US-Israeli war with Iran is having on energy markets, saying prices haven’t risen enough to trigger “meaningful demand destruction.”

item.title: Meta Hires Former Google, Stripe Executives Behind AI Startup Dreamer
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. has hired the founders and team behind the artificial intelligence startup Dreamer, which launched earlier this year to help people create their own AI agents.

item.title: US to Commit $250 Million Toward Supply Chain Investment Fund
item.description: The US plans to contribute $250 million toward an investment consortium that would help fund projects aimed at strengthening supply chains for energy and critical minerals, according to a Trump administration official.

item.title: JPMorgan Offers Clients a New Way to Hedge AI Debt Risk
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is offering clients a new way to bet against the debt of five hyperscalers, as investors seek more-liquid hedges amid an unprecedented borrowing spree to finance artificial-intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Sheinbaum Allies Rush to Lure Private Infrastructure Investment as Economy Sputters
item.description: President Claudia Sheinbaum’s allies in congress are in a hurry to lure more private investment by speeding up project approvals, clear-eyed that public spending alone isn’t sufficient to boost lackluster growth in Latin America’s second-biggest economy.

item.title: Apple to Start AI Comeback Bid on June 8 With Annual WWDC Conference
item.description: Apple Inc. announced plans to hold its annual Worldwide Developers Conference from June 8 to June 12, when the company is poised to unveil a make-or-break collection of AI features.

item.title: Bank of Canada Recruits New Officials as Kozicki, Mendes to Depart
item.description: The Bank of Canada is looking at internal candidates to fill spots on its rate-setting governing council after two deputy governors said they’ll leave the organization in the coming months.

item.title: Sheryl Sandberg Says Nscale and Its CEO Remind Her of Early Facebook
item.description: Sheryl Sandberg is back at a technology startup — and it reminds her of the halcyon days at Google and Facebook.

item.title: Israel Says Economy Lost Over $57 Billion During Two Years of War in Gaza
item.description: Israel lost 8.6% of annual gross domestic product, or 177 billion shekels ($57 billion), during the two years through 2025, figures that demonstrate the economic toll of the country’s state of near constant conflict.

item.title: HPE Assets Struggled With Buyer Interest in Antitrust Divestment
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. struggled to interest some investors and other tech companies in buying assets that the US government required be sold to clear its takeover of Juniper Networks, receiving bids that ranged between $1 and $15 million, according to court records.

item.title: Kalshi, Polymarket Founders Back New Prediction Market VC Fund
item.description: A new venture capital fund focused on prediction markets is launching with initial investments from a long list of prominent investors including two of the industry’s biggest players, Polymarket’s Shayne Coplan and Kalshi Inc.’s Tarek Mansour, according to an investment document viewed by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Citadel Securities’ Rubner Sees Short Squeeze Fueling US Stocks Rally
item.description: A record streak of short bets is at risk of unwinding, priming US equities for a comeback with hedge funds and systematic strategies poised to drive the next wave of buying, according to Citadel Securities’ Scott Rubner.

item.title: Apple Is Set to Put Ads in Apple Maps in Services Push
item.description: Apple Inc. is preparing to introduce advertising in its Maps app, part of a broader push to generate more money from services.

item.title: SpaceX Holder Fidelity Sees Path for Musk’s Data-in-Orbit Vision
item.description: Elon Musk’s bold vision for putting AI data centers in space, tapping into solar power and bypassing terrestrial worries such as electric grids and neighbors’ opposition, hinges on SpaceX’s ability to perfect its Starship booster.

item.title: Namibia Turns Down Starlink’s Bid to Operate in the Nation
item.description: Namibia turned down a bid by billionaire Elon Musk’s Starlink service to operate in the country, denying the satellite internet provider both a telecommunications service license and access to radio spectrum.
